Complete Workflow with hbsaems v0.3.0

Source: vignettes/complete-workflow.Rmd
complete-workflow.Rmd
library(hbsaems)

A typical Bayesian SAE workflow with hbsaems v0.3.0 follows seven steps, each backed by a single primary function.

1. Prior predictive check 

data("data_fhnorm")
model_prior <- hbm(
  formula      = brms::bf(y ~ x1 + x2 + x3),
  data         = data_fhnorm,
  sample_prior = "only",
  chains = 2, iter = 1000
)
pc <- prior_check(model_prior,
                  data         = data_fhnorm,
                  response_var = "y")
plot(pc)

2. Fit the model 

model <- hbm(
  formula = brms::bf(y ~ x1 + x2 + x3),
  data    = data_fhnorm,
  chains  = 4, iter = 4000, warmup = 2000, cores = 2
)
summary(model)

3. Convergence diagnostics 

diag <- convergence_check(model)
is_converged(model)
summary(diag)
plot(diag, type = "trace")
hbm_warnings(model)

4. Goodness-of-fit 

gof <- model_compare(model)         # single-model: LOO, WAIC, pp_check
summary(gof)
plot(gof, type = "pp_check")

5. Compare alternatives 

model2 <- hbm(brms::bf(y ~ x1 + x2), data = data_fhnorm,
              chains = 4, iter = 4000)
model3 <- hbm(brms::bf(y ~ x1),      data = data_fhnorm,
              chains = 4, iter = 4000)

model_compare(model, model2)
model_compare_all(full = model, medium = model2, simple = model3)

6. Small-area estimates 

est <- sae_predict(model)
summary(est)
plot(est, type = "predictions")
plot(est, type = "uncertainty")

# Bayesian model averaging
avg <- model_average(model, model2, weights = c(0.7, 0.3))

7. Post-processing predictions 

log_est  <- sae_transform(est, log)
sc_est   <- sae_scale(est)
hi_est   <- sae_filter(est, est$pred > stats::median(est$pred))
agg_est  <- sae_aggregate(sae_predict(model),
                          sae_predict(model2),
                          method = "mean")

On this page
